question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getHookStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an EQ key filter alongside a timestamp range to getHookStorage() such that findByKeyInAndTimestampBetween() returns a hook storage value that was written and then overwritten within the timestamp window, with the distinct on (key) ordering exposing an intermediate Hashgraph historical state rather than the final one?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an EVM address as the account ID to getNftAllowances() such that entityRepository.findByEvmAddress() resolves to a historically-deleted entity, causing the NFT allowance query to return allowance records from a Hashgraph history period when the entity was active?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user craft account.id and token.id range parameters to getNftAllowances() such that checkOwnerSpenderParamValidity() passes but the resulting JOOQ query in NftAllowanceRepositoryCustomImpl returns allowance records from a historical Hashgraph state where the allowance was already revoked?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user toggle the owner parameter in getNftAllowances() to switch between SPENDER and OWNER field queries, causing the SORT_ORDERS map in NftAllowanceRepositoryCustomImpl to select a sort order that exposes NFT allowance records from a different Hashgraph historical perspective than intended?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getOutstandingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted receiverId range to getOutstandingAirdrops() that causes processRequest() to build a Bound with overlapping range operators, bypassing the AirdropState.PENDING filter in TokenAirdropRepositoryCustomImpl and returning airdrop records from a historical Hashgraph state where the airdrop was already claimed?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getPendingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted senderId range to getPendingAirdrops() that causes the JOOQ query in TokenAirdropRepositoryCustomImpl to return token airdrop records from a historical Hashgraph period where the sender's account had a different balance, misrepresenting the historical airdrop state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSystemFile()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a timestamp parameter to getExchangeRate() or getFees() that causes the retryTemplate in FileServiceImpl to exhaust all 9 retries by targeting a timestamp range where all file_data records are malformed, ultimately throwing EntityNotFoundException and denying access to legitimate Hashgraph historical file data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSystemFile()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp to getExchangeRate() that causes the retryTemplate to fall back through multiple file versions via the upperBound.set(fileData.getConsensusTimestamp() - 1) decrement, ultimately serving an exchange rate from a much earlier Hashgraph historical period that misrepresents the current rate?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getFileAtTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a lowerTimestamp of 0 and an upperTimestamp of Long.MAX_VALUE to getFileAtTimestamp() via the timestamp parameter in getExchangeRate(), causing the string_agg query to concatenate all historical file_data chunks across the entire Hashgraph history and return a corrupted or unintended file?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getFileAtTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a timestamp range to getFileAtTimestamp() that targets a consensus_timestamp window containing only transaction_type=19 (append) records with non-empty file_data, causing string_agg to assemble a partial file that misrepresents the Hashgraph's historical system file state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getFileAtTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp range to getFileAtTimestamp() where the inner subquery (selecting the most recent transaction_type=17 or non-empty transaction_type=19) returns a consensus_timestamp that is different from what the outer query aggregates, causing a mismatch in the assembled Hashgraph historical file data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getLatestTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getLatestTimestamp() query in FileDataRepository by sending concurrent requests that cause the application to cache or use a stale latest timestamp, resulting in historical Hashgraph file data being served from an incorrect consensus point?
